title: NAP15 Overview
content: All Top 15 Alliances are part of the Non-Aggression Pact (NAP15). NAP15 alliance members are protected anywhere on the map.
Check Alliance > Settings > Alliance List > Alliance Rankings for the most updated NAP15 list.

changes.text: Some protections will also apply to non-NAP15 alliance members located inside of a territory touching the Capitol.

NAP15 alliance members are protected anywhere on the map, but protection for members of alliances outside the Top 15 will not apply to individual alliance members located outside of these territories. However, Enemy Buster Days should be focused on plundering from opponent servers rather than attacking 1586 users.

Example: If 80 members of an alliance are in a territory touching the Capitol and 20 are across the border in a different territory not touching the Capitol, the relevant NAP15 rules will only apply to the 80 members in the Capitol-adjacent territory.

title: Protected Territories
items: Bases belonging to NAP15 members or non-NAP15 members in a territory touching the Capitol cannot be hit or ashed
NAP15 members are protected anywhere on the map
Non-NAP15 bases outside protected territories may be attacked

title: Resource Protection
items: Trains, trucks, tasks, and resource tiles cannot be attacked except:
Tiles in the mud during Capitol War (or Fridays 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM ET if no Capitol War)
When both sides mutually agree (must be posted in alliance chat)

title: Information Security
items: Coordinates of NAP15 bases, trains, trucks, or tiles may NOT be shared with outside servers
No agreements with other servers to attack NAP15 resources
Cross-server agreements giving resources/territory to outsiders require Council approval

title: Discipline System
items: Eye-for-an-eye: Base hits and plundering are matched equivalently
Sharing coordinates: 1 day in jail (no loss) or 3 days (with loss)
Multiple violations: 1 week in jail
Outside server agreements: Alliance loses buffs for 3 days
Refusing punishment: Triple discipline on R5/R4

title: Insults & Malicious Language
items: No personal insults, profanity, name-calling, or threats in World Chat or DMs
First offense: 3 days to 2 weeks in Jail Alliance
Second offense: Ban from NAP15 protections
Threatening language: Immediate ban from NAP15

changes.text: Don't Be a Dick Clause - General Principle: All NAP15 members are expected to act with basic respect and good faith toward fellow server members. While competition is part of the game, intentionally antagonistic, petty, or disruptive behavior undermines server unity and cooperation
Prohibited Behavior: Deliberate trolling or baiting of other players in World Chat, alliance chats, or DMs
Exploiting technicalities in server rules to harass or disadvantage others while staying "technically legal"
Repeated petty grievances or manufacturing conflicts to create drama
Passive-aggressive behavior such as purposely blocking paths, camping in alliance hives, or other actions designed to frustrate without direct rule violations
Refusal to communicate in good faith when resolving disputes
Spreading rumors or misinformation about other alliances or players
Celebrating or gloating excessively after discipline actions against others
Interfering with gameplay in ways not explicitly prohibited but clearly meant to annoy (e.g., repeatedly scouting the same base, following players around unnecessarily)
"Don't Be a Dick" Discipline: First offense → Warning from alliance leadership and notification to Council
Second offense → 3-7 days in Jail Alliance
Third offense → 1-2 weeks in Jail Alliance
Continued violations → Ban from NAP15 protections

Spirit of the Rule: When in doubt, ask yourself: "Am I doing this to achieve a legitimate game objective, or am I just being a dick?" If it's the latter, don't do it. This clause is intentionally broad to address behaviors that may not fit other specific rules but clearly violate the spirit of server cooperation

title: Council Voting
items: R5s (or designees) from Top 5 alliances + 2 rotating from Top 6-15
Majority vote determines outcomes
Offended and Accused parties don't vote in disciplinary matters
Top 3 alliances break ties
24-hour response window or vote is forfeited

changes.text: Votes will be cast by the R5s (or designee) of the Top 5 Alliances in a separate R5 channel, plus 2 weekly rotating alliances from the Top 6–15
R5s (or designees) from Top 5 alliances + 2 rotating from Top 6-15
Each R5 eligible for voting may delegate to an R4. If an R5 chooses to delegate to an R4, the council should be notified via the 1586 Council Discord channel
If the topic is disciplinary, the Offended Party and Accused Party alliances do not vote
Offended and Accused parties don't vote in disciplinary matters
In the event of a tie, the Top 3 alliances break it
Top 3 alliances break ties
Example: If the 2nd and 5th ranked alliances cannot vote due to involvement in a disciplinary issue, the 1st, 3rd, and 4th alliances cast the tie-breaking vote
If a voting member or delegate does not respond within 24 hours, they forfeit their vote
24-hour response window or vote is forfeited

title: Presidency & Stability
items: Presidency rotates weekly among Top 3 alliances
New Top 3 alliances must hold rank for 7 consecutive days
New Top 15 alliances must hold rank for 7 consecutive days for NAP15 eligibility
New R5s must ratify rules within 7 days or lose NAP15 protections

title: Jail Alliance
items: Punishment system for serious infractions
Jail members protected under NAP15 but don't rotate in Council
Continued violations while in jail result in permanent ban from NAP15
Punishment follows members to new alliances if they leave

title: Conflict Resolution Process
items: Offended Party notifies Accused Party directly
If unresolved, escalate to R5/R4 with proof
Alliance leaders attempt to agree on discipline
If refused, escalate to Council with documentation
Council votes and Server President enforces outcome
Agreed actions must be communicated in alliance chats
